subject: DevOps
title: Zero Trust and Least Privilege
description: One-minute skill: Zero Trust and Least Privilege.

hook.text: The intern's account had admin access to prod. Nobody noticed until the accidental DELETE query.

buildup.text: Least privilege grants each identity only the minimum permissions needed. No more, no less.

discovery.text: Zero trust: never trust, always verify. Even internal services must prove identity on every request.

twist.text: Old networks trusted everything inside the firewall. Zero trust says the perimeter is irrelevant.

climax.text: Service meshes enforce zero trust with mutual TLS. Every service proves identity to every other one.

punchline.text: Trust nothing. Verify everything. Give minimum access needed.

quiz.question: What does 'zero trust' mean in security?
options: Never trust any identity by default; always verify authentication and authorization
Don't trust any employees with access
Only trust services running in the cloud
